name: Richard Wagner
abstract: Wilhelm Richard Wagner ( VAHG-nər; German: [ˈvɪlˌhɛlm ˈʁɪçaʁt ˈvaːɡnɐ] ; 22 May 1813 – 13 February 1883) was a German composer, theatre director, essayist, and conductor, best known for his operas, although his mature works are often referred to as music dramas. Unlike most composers, Wagner wrote both the libretti and the music for all …

about_quotes.speaker: Jason Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: He turns it off, and he says, “That’s Wagner.” It’s like, “Who?” “Richard Wagner, some Ring cycle.” “What is that?” “It’s this opera.” “Okay.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jason Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: I like to listen to opera, especially by Verdi and Puccini and Richard Strauss, Richard Wagner. I enjoy that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: I didn’t mention Richard Wagner – Richard Wagner is the real pronunciation – but the operas that he did
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: where Richard Wagner used to do that. You know, he would write a viola part or a violin part... And I learned that. I learned a lot of things from studying like that

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I had to learn, even from high school Richard Wagner and Strauss and Beethoven and all these things
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 17, 2018
